title: Maytenus annobonensis
taxonomy: Maytenus annobonensis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, family Celastraceae, and order Celastrales. It belongs to the genus Maytenus and is a species of flowering plant. The Celastraceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the staff vines or bittershrub family. This family is part of the larger clade of angiosperms, which includes the majority of flowering plant species. Maytenus annobonensis is a rare and endemic species found on the island of Annobón in Equatorial Guinea.
uses: Maytenus annobonensis has been reported to possess various medicinal properties due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Studies have shown that extracts from the plant exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, suggesting its potential in treating infections and promoting wound healing. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Maytenus annobonensis and to isolate its active compounds. However, its traditional use and potential bioactivity make it an area of interest for further scientific investigation.
benefits: Maytenus annobonensis, a plant native to the island of São Tomé and Principe, has been studied for its potential health benefits. Research suggests that the plant may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could aid in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. constituents: Maytenus annobonensis contains several active constituents, including maytansine, maytanprone, and maytansinol, which are diterpenoid alkaloids. These compounds have shown potential in various biological activities, including antitumor and antimalarial properties. Maytansine, in particular, has been studied for its ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells. The diterpenoid alkaloids present in Maytenus annobonensis may also exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential of these active constituents.

intro: Maytenus annobonensis is a species of plant in the family Celastraceae, commonly known as the Angolensis or Annonense. Research has shown that Maytenus annobonensis has potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anticancer properties, which are attributed to its ability to modulate the immune system and inhibit the growth of cancer cells. The bioactive constituents of Maytenus annobonensis include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Maytenus annobonensis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections.
